meaning: n.用词不当,[律](在诉讼等中)写错姓名(或地名)
examples: 'Villa' was something of a misnomer ; the place was no more than an old farmhouse.
Herbal "tea" is something of a misnomer because these drinks contain no tea at all.
The name "hydrogen bond" is something of a misnomer, as it is not a true bond but a particularly strong dipole-dipole attraction.
"'Regional' is a misnomer, " says Meyer S. Frucher, chief executive of the Philadelphia Stock Exchange.
Andy Woolley, from the National Union of Teachers, said the word free school is a "misnomer".
